relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Morning brings counsel, binding, and handing-over to Pilate.
Jesus answers the king question with You say it.
Many accusations are laid on him.
Pilate presses him to answer.
Silence makes Pilate marvel.
Festival release opens a choice.
Barabbas is bound with rebels and murder.
The crowd asks for the usual release.
Pilate offers the king of the Judeans.
He knows envy is underneath.
The priests stir the crowd toward Barabbas.
Pilate asks what to do with Jesus.
The cry becomes crucifixion.
The question of harm is drowned by the cry.
Crowd-satisfaction hands Jesus over.
Soldiers gather the cohort.
Purple and thorns mock kingship.
They hail him as king in derision.
Violence bows in false worship.
The mockery ends in leading him out to crucify.
Simon of Cyrene is compelled to carry the cross.
They bring him to Golgotha.
He refuses the medicated wine.
They crucify him and divide the garments.
The third hour marks the crucifixion.
The charge names him king.
He is crucified between two others.
A later manuscript connects this to being counted with lawless ones.
Passersby mock the temple saying.
They demand self-saving from the cross.
Leaders mock saving that does not save itself.
They demand sight before trust.
Darkness covers the land.
Jesus prays the psalm of forsakenness.
Some hear Elijah in the cry.
Sour wine is lifted while they wait for Elijah.
Jesus releases the great voice and breathes out.
The temple veil tears from top to bottom.
The centurion sees how he dies and confesses Son of the Divine.
Women witnesses watch from afar.
They had followed and served him from Galilee to Jerusalem.
Evening comes on Preparation before Sabbath.
Joseph of Arimathea boldly asks for the body.
Pilate confirms the death.
The body is granted to Joseph.
Jesus is wrapped, placed in rock, and sealed with stone.
Mary Magdalene and Mary watch where he is laid.
